raw_consumer_statement: I purchased a necklace : XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX from Macys for {$160.00} plus taxes using my Macys store credit card on XX/XX/XXXX as a XXXX present for my son. In the following four weeks, the necklace turned brown. The website description states that the necklace is 14k gold over sterling silver. Gold and silver do not turn brown. When the necklace turned brown, it also turned my sons neck green where it sat on his skin. I contacted Macys and explained the situation and they approved the return for a full refund and issued me a prepaid return shipping label. The item was shipped back to Macys on XX/XX/XXXX and on XX/XX/XXXX, Macys shipped the necklace back to me. I called the store back and they apologized and reissued me a new prepaid return shipping label and said they made notes on my account to accept the returned necklace and issue me a full refund back to my Macys credit card. However, on XX/XX/XXXX, Macys shipped the necklace back to me again but under a different customers name. I do not recognize the name in which the package was under. I called Macys again and spoke with one representative and two different managers who stated that Macys should have never accepted the return because it was returned after XX/XX/XXXX. Even though two other customer service representatives approved the return, that it did not matter because they updated their return policies and were no longer accepting these returns and the other representatives would be educated to the new policies to avoid further misinformation. I explained that the website has the item listed as 14k gold and sterling silver, but the item is not either of these metals. They denied the return. After this, I reviewed the description again on the Macys website and learned that this has been an ongoing problem for many other customers purchasing the same product and Macys denied them as well.
